div1.entryFree:
Roll, vb. 1) tr. a) to move in a circle, to turn round: --ing his greedy eyeballs in his head, Lucr. 368. b) to wrap round on itself, to form into a circular body: the snake --ed in a flowering bank, H6B III, 1, 228. Tit. II, 3, 13.
2) intr. a) to move circularly, by turning round on its axis: stands upon the --ing restless stone, H5 III, 6, 30. H5 III, 6, 30 put this stone a --ing, H8 V, 3, 104. Used of eyes: the poet's eye, in a fine frenzy --ing, Mids. V, 12. makes fearful action with wrinkled brows, with nods, with --ing eyes, John IV, 2, 192. when your eyes r. so, Oth. V, 2, 38.
b) to move in general; used of volumes of water: deep woes r. forward like a gentle flood, Lucr. 1118. And of eyes, == to look about: in Ajax' eyes blunt rage and rigour --ed, Lucr. 1398. an eye more bright than theirs, less false in --ing, Sonn. 20, 5. varying in subjects as the eye doth r. to every varied object in his glance, LLL V, 2, 774. make his eyeballs r. with wonted sight, Mids. III, 2, 369.
